evidence_status: historical early dg-08 snapshot; retained for topology provenance, not selected-current performance

selected_current.sampling_scope: traced RUN-first argmax ranking; production chunked-Gumbel decisions and 256K capacity pass separately
selected_current.metric_scope: warmed 256-token output-block throughput at K=48; not pure prefill or first-request TTFT

pure_prefill_current.contract: full 30-layer model, one max_seq_len=65536 build, prefill only, synchronized first execution per shape, DG_PREFILL_RAGGED_LONG=1

device: QB2 / bh-qbge-06 / P150x4, mesh [1,4], TP=4, fabric FABRIC_1D

workload.note: unit = denoise step over the 256-token canvas + commit, NOT per-token AR decode

precision_preserved: BF16 weights/activations/KV and the established BF16 ordered online-chunk self-conditioning and terminal-entropy arithmetic
diffusion_decisions_preserved: temperature 0.8->0.4, Gumbel-max, entropy-budget accept (sort/cumsum/scatter), random-token renoise, commit = clean argmax

ttft_ms_reduced_layer.note: reduced-layer prefill of a 32-token prompt; scales ~linearly with layers

terminal_decision_step_traced_ms.note: 1-capture/20-replay warmed traced replay at real [1,1,256,262144]; argmax RUN-first path. Original is untraceable (ttnn.full write TT_FATAL during capture).

denoise_step_full_30L.measured: TRACED warmed replay, reduced-layer L=1/2/4 linear fit (traced ~= eager, path is op-cost bound); full denoise step captures + replays as a Metal trace

per_block.commit_note: 256 sequential single-token decode-appends; known functional-only path (plan future perf item; candidate: single causal 256-token commit forward)

roofline_ms_per_step_estimate.measured_vs_roofline: traced ~4176 ms/step is ~85-170x the bandwidth roofline -> denoise per-layer is op-count bound (manual chunked RoPE, staged GQA fallback, chunked norms), not DRAM-bandwidth bound; traced ~= eager (~3%) confirms op-cost not dispatch-gap

lever1_traced_decode.note: measured serving path (serving.decode_block -> denoise_block) is eager with 5 host readbacks/step + torch.equal halt; readback is a fixed ~28ms/step = 0.66% of the 30L step; a fixed-48-step trace would also remove the eager early-halt. Whole-run tracy GAP_FRACTION=81% is a device-profiler artifact, not real host gap.

moe_clean_microbench_bf16_256canvas.conclusion: dense-128 experts forward is ~99% of the per-layer denoise step and ~65-85x above BOTH weight and compute rooflines -> data-movement/transpose-reorder bound (expert-major transpose ~87ms + 8-chunk overhead), NOT weight- or compute-bound. Corrects the earlier RoPE/GQA/norm attribution.

ruled_out_quick_levers.bfp8_bfp4_expert_precision: won't help (weight traffic only ~1.6ms/layer; MoE is transpose-bound)
ruled_out_quick_levers.per_token_nnz8_sparse_on_256: BLOCKED: TT_FATAL sparsity.logical_volume()==batch_length (sparse_matmul sparsity is per M-tile-group, not per-token)

remaining_levers_scoped.true_sparse_token_gather_moe: biggest lever; sort tokens by expert into per-expert batches, dense matmul per expert, scatter weighted; cuts transpose+compute ~4-8x; RISK: on-device gather/scatter overhead may wash out (against the TT dense-all-experts grain); prototype+measure before full rewrite
remaining_levers_scoped.commit_batching_256decodes_to_1_prefill_: ~7x on the 31.5s commit (~1.25x block at 25 steps); needs DiffusionGemma-local causal 256-token prefill-append at offset start_pos, verified by KV-cache PCC; also skip discarded commit LM-head (~3% of commit)

lever_A_token_gather_moe_LANDED_2026_07_.approach: GShard capacity dispatch built ON-DEVICE from router topk (topk+scatter+cumsum+gather+col math, all trace-safe); gather via disp^T@x matmul; batched matmul over active experts (one C-token tile/expert); combine via comb@down matmul; TP all-reduce. Input TP-replicated -> gather/scatter local per device.

denoise_step_traced_ms.note: same-harness prof_denoise_step.py, warmed Metal trace, iters=5, L=2/4 linear fit. Both share F=50.2ms.

correctness.real_expert_load: max 25-31, mean 16, over-32=0 -> capacity 32 drops 0 pairs
correctness.note: exact method when 0 dropped; the layer delta is bf16 kernel rounding (batched matmul vs sparse_matmul) amplified via post-MoE RMSNorm. Smaller than the model's existing ~0.93-vs-fp32 bf16 error (fidelity #48291, deferred).

at_48_steps.note: denoise 48*461=22138ms + commit 31432ms; block now COMMIT-BOUND (59%) -> Lever B is the next critical path

lever_A_token_gather_moe_LANDED_2026_07_.trace_safety: constant/scratch buffers (ones/zeros/full) preallocated + cached outside the trace (in-trace allocation raises 'Writes not supported during trace capture'); ttnn.scatter verified out-of-place so zero-base reuse is correct; index tensors must be UINT32.
lever_A_token_gather_moe_LANDED_2026_07_.run_smoke_sparse_on: DG_TEXT_DEMO_SUCCESS generated_tokens=256 blocks=1 (4-layer, 8-step end-to-end run with DG_SPARSE_MOE=1)

lever_B_commit_batching_SCOPED_2026_07_0.verdict: SCOPED + QUANTIFIED, not landed (verify KV before keeping)
lever_B_commit_batching_SCOPED_2026_07_0.current_commit: 256 sequential single-token decode-appends (commit_canvas_tokens -> commit_decode_forward), full 30L weight traffic paid 256x; commit_ms_30L ~= 31432
lever_B_commit_batching_SCOPED_2026_07_0.batched_form: ONE causal prefill-append of the 256 committed tokens at offset start_pos (each attends to frozen prefix + earlier block tokens causally, writes K/V). Compute = a 256-token forward = denoise-step-equivalent.
blockers_found: prepare_inputs_prefill discards start_pos; Gemma4 computes prefill RoPE internally at pos 0.. -> stock prefill can't append at offset. RoPE offset IS controllable via cos/sin caches (denoise does this) -> feasible.

short-seq prefill SDPA (scaled_dot_product_attention is_causal=True) attends only to the current chunk, NOT the prefix cache; correct append needs chunked_prefill_sdpa (prefix-attending) + an offset KV write (paged_fill_cache/paged_update_cache at start_pos); smoke path uses a contiguous non-paged cache with no offset-fill primitive.
landing a wrong KV-write silently corrupts the cache -> a KV-content-PCC gate vs the decode path is mandatory before keeping.
lever_B_commit_batching_SCOPED_2026_07_0.build_spec: DiffusionGemma-local commit_prefill.py: commit_prefill_append(tt_model, canvas_tokens, start_pos) = embed 256 -> per layer {norm, causal prefix-attending SDPA at offset RoPE, offset KV write, sparse MoE, norms, PLI} -> skip LM head; gate on cache-K/V PCC vs 256-decode path.

named_limitations: CORRECTED 2026-07-04: the per-layer denoise cost is the dense-128 experts forward (137.60 ms/layer, clean non-profiled microbench), NOT chunked RoPE/GQA/norms as previously stated. It is transpose/data-movement bound, ~65-85x above weight & compute rooflines. Precision/trace/chunk-size/L1 levers all washed out or blocked; only token-gather true-sparse MoE or commit batching remain.

hardware-profiler-limited: build_Release has ENABLE_TRACY=OFF (confirmed CMakeCache); TT_METAL_DEVICE_PROFILER and python -m tracy raise 'requires a Tracy-enabled build'. tt-perf-report op-level CSV unavailable without a full ttnn rebuild that would disrupt the shared venv. Metal TRACE capture/replay itself works, so per-step/per-block/full-generation numbers ARE from a traced measured path; only the Tracy op-attribution table is substituted by synchronized per-op device-time tables.

backbone per-layer denoise (137.55 ms/layer traced) is op-count bound from manual chunked RoPE, staged GQA fallback, and chunked norms; identified as the dominant next optimization target (DiffusionGemma-local, ~3-5x headroom to the bandwidth roofline).
